Picture Support

Complete documentation for pictures in Aspose.Cells FOSS for .NET

← Back to Index

Picture Overview

The Picture class represents a picture (image) anchored to a worksheet with support for JPEG, PNG, GIF, and BMP formats.

Supported Image Formats

Aspose.Cells FOSS automatically detects image format from file extension or data bytes:

  • JPEG: .jpg, .jpeg
  • PNG: .png
  • GIF: .gif
  • BMP: .bmp

Usage Examples

Adding Pictures from Files

using Aspose.Cells_FOSS;
using System.IO;

var workbook = new Workbook();
var worksheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];

// Add picture from file path
byte[] imageData = File.ReadAllBytes("logo.png");
int pictureIndex = worksheet.Pictures.Add(5, 5, 10, 10, imageData);

// Add picture from file with different extension
byte[] jpegData = File.ReadAllBytes("photo.jpg");
int jpegIndex = worksheet.Pictures.Add(15, 5, 20, 15, jpegData);

// Add picture from GIF
byte[] gifData = File.ReadAllBytes("animation.gif");
int gifIndex = worksheet.Pictures.Add(25, 5, 30, 15, gifData);

Adding Pictures from Streams

using Aspose.Cells_FOSS;
using System.IO;

var workbook = new Workbook();
var worksheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];

// Add picture from stream
using (var stream = File.OpenRead("image.png"))
{
    int streamIndex = worksheet.Pictures.Add(35, 5, 40, 15, stream);
}

// Add multiple pictures from same stream
stream.Position = 0;
for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++)
{
    byte[] data = new byte[1000]; // Read first 1KB
    int bytesRead = stream.Read(data, 0, data.Length);
    int pictureIndex = worksheet.Pictures.Add(45 + (i * 15), 5 + (i * 15), 45 + (i * 15), data);
}

Console.WriteLine($"Added {worksheet.Pictures.Count} pictures from stream");

Adding Pictures from Byte Arrays

using Aspose.Cells_FOSS;

var workbook = new Workbook();
var worksheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];

// Create a simple image byte array (100x100 red pixels)
byte[] redImage = new byte[10000];
for (int i = 0; i < redImage.Length; i++)
{
    redImage[i] = 255; // Red
}

// Add picture from byte array
int pictureIndex = worksheet.Pictures.Add(10, 10, 25, 15, redImage);

Console.WriteLine($"Added picture at index {pictureIndex}");

Picture Properties

The Picture class provides the following properties and methods:

  • Name - Display name of the picture
  • ImageType - Detected image format (JPEG, PNG, GIF, BMP, Unknown)
  • UpperLeftRow - Zero-based row of upper-left anchor
  • UpperLeftColumn - Zero-based column of upper-left anchor
  • LowerRightRow - Zero-based row of lower-right anchor
  • LowerRightColumn - Zero-based column of lower-right anchor
  • Data - Raw image byte array
  • Size - Picture size (width and height in EMU)

PictureCollection

The PictureCollection class manages all pictures in a worksheet:

  • Count - Number of pictures
  • this[int] - Access picture by index
  • Add(byte[]) - Add from byte array
  • Add(Stream) - Add from file stream
  • Add(string) - Add from file path
  • RemoveAt() - Remove picture at index
  • Parameters: position coordinates, image data, or file path

ImageType Enumeration

Enumeration of supported image formats:

  • Jpeg: JPEG image format
  • Png: PNG image format
  • Gif: GIF image format
  • Bmp: BMP image format
  • Unknown: Unrecognized image format

Important Notes

  • Automatic Format Detection: Image type is automatically detected from file extension or data magic bytes
  • Anchor Positioning: Pictures are positioned using anchor coordinates (upper-left and lower-right)
  • Stream Support: Can load from file path, stream, or byte array
  • Memory Management: Large images may require memory consideration
